input.title: Agent Trace Discipline
input.article_excerpt: A trace is more than a log line. For self-improving workflows, the trace must preserve task inputs, intermediate actions, outputs, scores, and failure signals so later runs can be compared against the same task set.
input.summary_goal: Explain why structured traces are the practical base layer for evaluation and reflection.

input.title: Source Status Drift
input.article_excerpt: When notes merge official facts, paper claims, community implementations, and author inference into one voice, later reflection notes can optimize for the wrong kind of confidence.
input.summary_goal: Explain why source-status discipline matters before building memory.

input.title: Memory Leakage Risk
input.article_excerpt: A memory-enhanced run is not persuasive if the memory contains task-specific answers from the evaluation set. The memory should store reusable rules, not held-out article facts or final responses.
input.summary_goal: Explain how leakage can make a memory experiment look better than it is.
